variants: Elida Scholl holds a Petrified Woodcarver Seal, which is a professional license recognizing her expertise in the art of wood carving from petrified wood. She is a graduate of the Sesame Grove Institute, where she developed her skills and appreciation for natural artistry. Beyond her creative pursuits, Elida serves as the Host of Periwinkle Creek Symposium, a civic role that allows her to engage with her community and promote artistic awareness. A noteworthy part of her career involved publishing her work with Emerald Wisp House, contributing to the world of art literature. Elida also communicates fluently in Vietnamese tones, which enriches her interactions both personally and professionally.
